description: Documentation Accuracy & Codebase Alignment - Systematically align all docs pages with the live codebase by fixing discrepancies, removing redundancy, and standardizing structure

userStories.title: Fix API reference page against controller source
userStories.description: As a user integrating with the Roombarr API, I want the documented response schemas in reference/api.md to exactly match what the server actually returns.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the `error` field from the GET /evaluate/:runId failed-response documentation (the controller does not serialize it; the docs were incorrect)
Do not document `services_unavailable` (it exists in the EvaluationRun type but is never populated or serialized by the controller)

Mark `execution_status` and `execution_error` as optional fields in the EvaluationItemResult table (matching their `?` type declarations) with a note explaining they are absent before the executor runs
Add a note that `actions_executed.keep` is always `0` in live mode (the executor skips keep items before counting)
Update all example JSON responses in api.md to match the actual controller output shapes (cross-reference src/evaluation/evaluation.controller.ts lines 53-70 for the exact fields serialized)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/evaluation/evaluation.controller.ts, src/evaluation/evaluation.types.ts, src/rules/types.ts

userStories.title: Fix operators reference page null handling gaps
userStories.description: As a user writing rule conditions, I want the operators reference to accurately document null handling for all operators and edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Document what happens when the `value` (not the field) is null or non-array for `includes_all` — code returns `false` silently because `Array.isArray(null)` is false (src/rules/operators.ts)
Clarify that `is_empty` and `is_not_empty` silently ignore any extra `value` key in the condition — the operator functions only take one argument and the value is not validated away at config time
Verify all other operator null-handling claims in the table match the implementations in src/rules/operators.ts — no other corrections needed based on audit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/rules/operators.ts. The equals/not_equals null passthrough and not_includes behavior are already accurate.

userStories.title: Fix fields reference page and add missing fields
userStories.description: As a user writing rules, I want the fields reference to list every available field and accurately describe operator compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `radarr.path` (type: string) to the Radarr fields section with a description like 'Filesystem path to the movie folder'
Add `sonarr.path` (type: string) to the Sonarr fields section with a description like 'Filesystem path to the series folder'
Update the field count in any summary text to reflect the new total (was 28, now 30 with the two path fields)

Update the State fields section: replace 'currently Radarr-only' with nuanced framing — the state tracking system is generic and supports any target, but the only state fields defined today (state.days_off_import_list, state.ever_on_import_list) track Radarr-specific data

Add an operator compatibility table to this page showing which operators work with each field type (string, number, boolean, date, array) — this will become the canonical home for operator-to-field-type compatibility, referenced by integration pages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/config/field-registry.ts, src/snapshot/state-registry.ts. This page becomes the canonical home for operator compatibility tables (per US-008 decision).

userStories.title: Fix environment variables reference page
userStories.description: As a user configuring Roombarr's container, I want the environment variables reference to accurately describe each variable's behavior and edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Visually separate PUID and PGID into a 'Container-level variables' section (or similar) distinct from application-level variables (PORT, CONFIG_PATH, DB_PATH, NODE_ENV) — make clear these are handled by the LinuxServer.io base image, not the Node.js application

Fix CONFIG_PATH fallback description: if CONFIG_PATH is set but the file does not exist, Roombarr silently falls through to /config/roombarr.yml rather than refusing to start. Only if both paths are missing does it throw. (Cross-reference src/config/config.service.ts CONFIG_PATHS array and the for-loop logic)

Fix NODE_ENV description: say 'any value other than production' triggers debug/pretty mode — not specifically 'development'. The code checks `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` (src/app.module.ts)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/main.ts, src/config/config.service.ts, src/app.module.ts

userStories.title: Fix config validation claims in overview and rules docs
userStories.description: As a user writing a roombarr.yml, I want the docs to accurately describe what the config validator accepts and rejects.
acceptanceCriteria: In rules.md, remove the claim that rule `name` must be unique — the Zod schema only enforces z.string().min(1), no uniqueness check exists

In overview.md, describe `schedule` as accepting a standard 5-field cron expression without implying that invalid cron strings are rejected at config load — the schema accepts any non-empty string; invalid cron fails at runtime when the scheduler tries to parse it

In rules.md, document the field path regex constraint (/^[a-z][a-z0-9_.]*$/) under the leaf condition anatomy — field paths must start with a lowercase letter and contain only lowercase letters, digits, underscores, and dots
In rules.md, update any 'Radarr-only' state field references to match the nuanced framing from US-003
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/config/config.schema.ts (Zod schemas for ruleSchema, leafConditionSchema, configSchema)

userStories.title: Update example config file
userStories.description: As a new user copying the example config, I want roombarr.example.yml to be complete, accurate, and consistently annotated.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `dry_run: true` as an explicit top-level field (currently missing, silently defaults)
Update audit.retention_days comment to include 'Max: 3650' alongside the existing 'Min: 1'
Update performance.concurrency comment to include '1–50' range and 'Default: 10'
Add a comment near the top referencing the docs site for details (e.g., '# Full documentation: https://roombarr.github.io/roombarr/')
Verify the example config is valid against the current Zod schema by reviewing all field names and structure against src/config/config.schema.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix Radarr integration page accuracy and structure
userStories.description: As a user setting up Radarr rules, I want the Radarr integration page to be accurate against the source code and follow a consistent structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Change `radarr.status` field description to use 'e.g.' phrasing (like Sonarr does) instead of presenting tba/announced/inCinemas/released as an exhaustive list — the Radarr API may return other values like 'deleted'
Add `radarr.path` (type: string, description: 'Filesystem path to the movie folder') to the field table
Replace the full 'Tags are lowercased' explanation with a one-line note + link to the canonical location (reference/fields.md or configuration/rules.md)
Replace the full 'Null dates' subsection with a one-sentence note linking to reference/operators.md § Null handling
Replace the inline 'Compatible operators' content with a link to reference/fields.md (the new canonical home for operator compatibility)
Add the startup validation warning (Roombarr refuses to start if rules reference radarr.* fields but services.radarr is missing) — currently only on Jellyfin/Jellyseerr pages
Ensure section order matches the standard: Setup → Evaluation model → Fields → Tags → Operators → Actions → Example rules
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/radarr/, src/config/field-registry.ts. Must be done after US-003 (fields.md canonical home exists).

userStories.title: Fix Sonarr integration page accuracy and structure
userStories.description: As a user setting up Sonarr rules, I want the Sonarr integration page to be accurate and structurally consistent with other integration pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `sonarr.path` (type: string, description: 'Filesystem path to the series folder') to the field table
Add at least one `keep` rule example (the page currently only shows delete and unmonitor examples, unlike Radarr which demonstrates all three actions)
Replace the full 'Tags are lowercased' explanation with a one-line note + link to the canonical location
Replace the inline 'Compatible operators' content with a link to reference/fields.md
Add the startup validation warning (Roombarr refuses to start if rules reference sonarr.* fields but services.sonarr is missing)
Ensure section order matches the standard: Setup → Per-season evaluation model → Fields → Tags → Operators → Actions → Example rules
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source of truth: src/sonarr/, src/config/field-registry.ts. Must be done after US-003 (fields.md canonical home exists).

userStories.title: Fix Jellyfin integration page accuracy and structure
userStories.description: As a user setting up Jellyfin enrichment, I want the Jellyfin integration page to be accurate about edge cases and structurally consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the `totalActiveUsers > 0` guard on `jellyfin.watched_by_all`: when there are zero active Jellyfin users, watched_by_all is always false (cross-reference src/jellyfin/jellyfin.aggregator.ts)
Remove the embedded 'Compatible operators' table and replace with a link to reference/fields.md
Replace the full 'Missing data behavior' / 'rules skipped' explanation with a one-line cross-reference to the canonical location (how-it-works.md or configuration/rules.md)
Add a :::tip connectivity note linking to Docker networking docs (currently only on Radarr/Sonarr pages)
Ensure section order matches the standard: Setup → Evaluation model → Fields → Operators → Missing data → Example rules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix Jellyseerr integration page accuracy and structure
userStories.description: As a user setting up Jellyseerr enrichment, I want the Jellyseerr integration page to be accurate and structurally consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify the API key path matches between jellyseerr.md ('Settings → General → API Key') and troubleshooting.md — fix whichever is inconsistent so both say the same thing
Remove the embedded 'Compatible operators' table and replace with a link to reference/fields.md
Replace the full 'Missing data behavior' / 'rules skipped' explanation with a one-line cross-reference to the canonical location
Replace the full null-date caution admonition with a one-sentence note linking to reference/operators.md § Null handling
Add a :::tip connectivity note linking to Docker networking docs (currently only on Radarr/Sonarr pages)
Ensure section order matches the standard: Setup → Evaluation model → Fields → Operators → Missing data → Example rules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix actions.md, troubleshooting.md, and how-it-works.md
userStories.description: As a user navigating the docs, I want supporting pages to use accurate wording, consistent cross-references, and no duplicated reference material.
acceptanceCriteria: In actions.md, clarify unmonitor wording: 'Stops the target service (Radarr or Sonarr) from searching for new downloads' — removing the ambiguous 'Roombarr's target service' phrasing
In troubleshooting.md, ensure all API key paths match their corresponding integration pages exactly (especially Jellyseerr: 'Settings → General → API Key')
In how-it-works.md, verify it remains a conceptual overview — if any reference-level detail duplicates reference pages, replace with cross-references
Ensure conflict resolution priority (keep > unmonitor > delete) in how-it-works.md and recipes.md links to configuration/actions.md rather than fully restating the chain
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final consistency pass across supporting pages. Must be done after integration pages (US-007 through US-010) are updated.

userStories.title: Validate docs build with no broken links
userStories.description: As a docs maintainer, I want to confirm that all cross-reference changes produce a clean build with no broken links.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npm run docs:build` from the repository root and confirm it completes with zero errors
Confirm starlight-links-validator reports no broken internal links
If any broken links are found, fix them — they were likely introduced by heading anchor changes or page restructuring in previous stories
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must be the final story — validates all prior changes. The starlight-links-validator plugin catches broken internal links at build time.
